evidence: Methods: "We implemented the random forest analysis using the ranger package...We used random forest permutations to determine importance scores." Figure 2 and 4 captions confirm "variable importance mode: permutation." Results report: "The permutation-based variable importance scores of the different predictors are: infant mortality score: 0.25; life expectancy score: 0.19; maternal mortality score: 0.17." The paper explicitly employs permutation-based importance, not impurity importance, and therefore is not vulnerable to the bias described.
